probes.name: goal_stats_with_streaks
probes.prompt: Run a focused data gather on my goals and habit streaks. I want to see: which goals are closest to completion, which habits have the longest current streak, and whether any habits were skipped in the last 7 days. Present the raw numbers in a structured format.
probes.criteria:
The funfact/data_gatherer agent should execute 3-5 targeted SQL queries against goal and habit tables, returning structured numerical data (goal progress percentages, streak counts, skip counts). A good response avoids guessing or fabricating data, runs specific queries rather than enumerating all tables, and outputs clean structured results ready for a pattern analyst.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 5, 22, 33 — user asking for interesting observations from personal data, goal/habit analysis.

probes.name: chat_activity_summary
probes.prompt: Zebraj dane o mojej aktywności w czacie z ostatnich 14 dni: ile wiadomości wysłałem, w które dni byłem najbardziej aktywny, i jaka była średnia długość moich wiadomości. Podaj wyniki w ustrukturyzowanym formacie.
probes.criteria:
The funfact/data_gatherer agent should run targeted SQL queries on chat activity tables for the last 14 days, returning message counts per day, peak activity days, and average message length. A good response provides concrete numbers from queries, not prose summaries, and structures the output clearly for downstream analysis.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: todo_completion_rate
probes.prompt: Pull my todo completion stats: how many todos were created vs completed in the last 30 days, the completion rate percentage, and which categories of todos have the lowest completion rate. I need the structured numbers, not commentary.
probes.criteria:
The funfact/data_gatherer agent should query the todo/completion tables to extract creation counts, completion counts, completion rate, and per-category breakdowns. A good response is data-focused with precise numbers, covers 3-5 queries max without over-exploring the schema, and formats results for the pattern analyst agent.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.name: cross_domain_correlation
probes.prompt: Check if there's any correlation in my data between days when I complete more todos and days when I have longer habit streaks or send more chat messages. Pull the relevant counts for the last 30 days — todos completed per day, habit check-ins per day, and messages per day — so the correlation can be computed. Just give me the structured data.
probes.criteria:
The funfact/data_gatherer agent should run focused SQL queries pulling daily aggregates from todo, habit, and chat tables over 30 days, returning structured day-by-day or summarized counts. A good response does not attempt to compute correlations itself (that is the analyst's job), but ensures all three data dimensions are present and aligned by date, within the 3-5 query budget.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 3 about correlation processes via LLM, and message 33 about analyzing data for patterns.

probes.name: recent_jumble_overview
probes.prompt: Daj mi szybki przegląd: ile mam aktywnych celów, ile nawyków z nieprzerwanym streakiem powyżej 7 dni, i ile todo z deadlinesem w przyszłym tygodniu jest jeszcze niezrobionych. Same liczby, uporządkowane.
probes.criteria:
The funfact/data_gatherer agent should execute exactly 3 targeted queries — active goals count, habits with streak > 7 days, and incomplete todos with deadlines in the next 7 days — and return the raw structured numbers. A good response is concise, does not enumerate tables or explore the schema, and provides the three requested metrics in a clean format for the pattern analyst.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
